target.title: fix(mcp): kill orphaned stdio children on session teardown + auto-reconnect dead transports
target.body:
## Problem

Two MCP stability issues causing cron failures and mid-turn `Not connected` errors:

1. **Orphaned MCP child processes:** When isolated cron sessions end, the CLI runner only closes the HTTP loopback server but leaves `SessionMcpRuntime` stdio children alive until the idle sweep. The `server-cron.ts` isolated wrapper also only cleans browser sessions. This causes process accumulation (observed 78+ orphaned processes between sweeps).

2. **Unrecoverable transport death:** When an MCP child process dies mid-session (OOM, crash, pipe break), the stdio transport's `send()` throws `Not connected` because `stdin` is gone. This error is unrecoverable for the entire turn, even though the server config is still valid and a fresh process could be spawned.

## Fix

### Commit 1: Kill MCP stdio children on isolated session teardown

- `server-cron.ts`: `runIsolatedAgentJob` finally block now also retires the MCP runtime for isolated cron sessions via `retireSessionMcpRuntimeForSessionKey()`
- `cli-runner.ts`: when `cleanupBundleMcpOnRunEnd` is true, retires MCP runtime by session key (falling back to session ID) after `closeMcpLoopbackServer()`

### Commit 2: Auto-reconnect dead MCP server sessions on tool call failure

- `agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts`: `callTool()` now catches transport-death errors (`Not connected`, `EPIPE`, `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`, `write after end`) and attempts a single reconnection: dispose dead session → resolve fresh transport → connect → retry tool call
- If reconnection fails, the original error is thrown unchanged
- Reconnection is logged at warn level for observability

### Commit 3: Separate reconnect failure from retried tool-call error (ClawSweeper review fix)

Split the inner try/catch into two phases per ClawSweeper's [P2] finding:
- **Phase 1** (try/catch): `connectWithTimeout` only. If this fails, dispose the new session and throw the original transport-death error.
- **Phase 2** (unguarded): retry `client.callTool` on the healthy reconnected session. If this fails, it's a normal MCP error from a live server, not a transport issue, so it propagates directly.

This ensures `sessions` map consistency: a healthy reconnected session is never disposed by a subsequent call failure.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Orphaned MCP child accumulation (before fix)

Live capture from production OpenClaw instance (Mac Studio M1 Max, 32 GB) showing 3 waves of duplicate MCP children under gateway PID 70237, each spawned by successive cron runs without cleanup:

```
=== BEFORE: Current MCP child processes ===
Timestamp: 2026-05-30T00:18:57Z

MCP child process counts by server:
  PID=46292 PPID=70237 RSS=81104KB  ETIME=03:15 proton-pass
  PID=46300 PPID=70237 RSS=117664KB ETIME=03:14 playwright-public
  PID=46301 PPID=70237 RSS=29344KB  ETIME=03:14 cua-driver
  PID=46309 PPID=70237 RSS=116368KB ETIME=03:14 playwright-logged-in
  PID=46316 PPID=70237 RSS=48160KB  ETIME=03:14 threads-mcp
  PID=46317 PPID=70237 RSS=69120KB  ETIME=03:14 veritas

  PID=46336 PPID=70237 RSS=81104KB  ETIME=02:48 proton-pass (wave 2)
  PID=46345 PPID=70237 RSS=116240KB ETIME=02:47 playwright-public
  PID=46346 PPID=70237 RSS=29312KB  ETIME=02:47 cua-driver
  PID=46354 PPID=70237 RSS=115024KB ETIME=02:47 playwright-logged-in
  PID=46361 PPID=70237 RSS=48048KB  ETIME=02:47 threads-mcp
  PID=46362 PPID=70237 RSS=69360KB  ETIME=02:47 veritas

  PID=46410 PPID=70237 RSS=99248KB  ETIME=01:23 proton-pass (wave 3)
  PID=46418 PPID=70237 RSS=147184KB ETIME=01:23 playwright-public
  PID=46419 PPID=70237 RSS=29264KB  ETIME=01:22 cua-driver
  PID=46426 PPID=70237 RSS=135376KB ETIME=01:22 playwright-logged-in
  PID=46433 PPID=70237 RSS=47904KB  ETIME=01:22 threads-mcp
  PID=46434 PPID=70237 RSS=79184KB  ETIME=01:22 veritas

Summary: 6 servers × 3 waves = 18 orphaned MCP children, ~1.2 GB RSS
Total MCP-like children: 25 (including non-gateway-parented)
```

Three distinct etime cohorts (03:14, 02:47, 01:22) confirm each cron run spawned a full set of 6 MCP servers and left the previous set alive.

### Config-level mitigation (already deployed)

In addition to the code fix, 11 cron jobs were updated with `toolsAllow` scoping so they only spawn the MCP servers they actually need. Example: `mcp-orphan-cleanup` only runs a python script via `exec`, so `toolsAllow: ["exec", "process"]` prevents it from spawning 6 MCP servers at all.

### CI failure note

The `checks-node-core-fast` failure is `src/security/audit-sandbox-browser.test.ts` (2 tests timing out at 120s waiting for Docker probes). Unrelated to this PR; no MCP files touched by those tests.

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- Rolling timer suffix shows elapsed time while running, cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Depends on

- #80046 (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args)
- #85381 (CLI thinking_delta events, stacked on #80046)

**Merge order is safe.** The feature is default-off and consumes only existing callbacks. With the interactive backend's restored thinking, reasoning arrives on `onReasoningStream`; without #85381 (thinking redacted), CLI reasoning simply never reaches the renderer and the lane shows tool/event lines without CLI-native reasoning — degrading gracefully rather than breaking. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected, so this can merge before or after #85381.

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (unwrap `<think>` markers, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, defensive final-answer strip, reasoning-tag unwrapping (incl. literal tags inside code preserved)
- [ ] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Live proof (current head)

_Redacted current-head Telegram proof for the cases below. (The earlier screenshot predated the reply-out / continuation / empty-flush changes and is superseded.)_

- **Normal interleaving** — reasoning + tool-use lines in the Thinking lane:
  <!-- add screenshot -->
- **No duplicate final answer** — the reply appears once, as its own message, not inside the Thinking lane:
  <!-- add screenshot -->
- **Long-message continuation** — a long turn spills the Thinking lane into a second message instead of freezing:
  <!-- add screenshot -->
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn with no reasoning/tool content emits no bare "Thinking" message:
  <!-- add screenshot -->

target.title: fix(agents): compact lean local tool catalogs
target.body:
## Summary

- Defaults `localModelLean` runs to structured Tool Search controls when `tools.toolSearch` is not explicitly configured.
- Preserves explicit operator Tool Search config, including `tools.toolSearch: false` opt-out.
- Threads the lean effective config into embedded runner Tool Search setup and documents the behavior for local model/Ollama users.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 3 files, 122 tests on final pushed SHA `a9b6ac4f121`.
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed on final pushed SHA `a9b6ac4f121`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.ts` passed on final pushed SHA `a9b6ac4f121`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.ts` passed on final pushed SHA `a9b6ac4f121`.
- Live Ollama Cloud source-level stream probe with structured Tool Search control tools passed for `gemma3:12b`, `rnj-1:8b`, and `gpt-oss:20b`; each payload contained `tool_search`, `tool_describe`, `tool_call`, `think: false`, `num_ctx: 4096`, and returned `OK`.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed with exit 0 before final moving-main rebases: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_839cfc1fefcb`, run `run_8b6272786459`, commit `104362e5233` rebased on `0e6937cc1be`. Later rebases to `af42e2d309c` and `a9b6ac4f121` were conflict-free and followed by the targeted/local checks above.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` passed clean: no accepted/actionable findings; overall patch-correctness score 0.84.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: local-model lean runs now compact larger tool catalogs behind structured Tool Search controls by default, reducing prompt/tool schema pressure for smaller models while preserving explicit operator config.

Real environment tested: local macOS node-wrapper Vitest/static checks; Ollama Cloud live stream probes with hosted small models; AWS Crabbox Linux c7a.8xlarge changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node --import tsx` source-level Ollama Cloud stream probe using `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts`; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` in run `run_8b6272786459`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`.

Evidence after fix: focused test run passed 122 tests on final pushed SHA `a9b6ac4f121`; live Ollama probes accepted the structured `tool_search` / `tool_describe` / `tool_call` surface and returned `OK`; Crabbox changed gate passed with exit 0 in `run_8b6272786459` before the final conflict-free rebases; autoreview reported no actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: lean mode injects `{ enabled: true, mode: "tools", searchDefaultLimit: 5, maxSearchLimit: 10 }` only when Tool Search is unset; explicit `tools.toolSearch` config remains untouched; hosted Ollama models accept the resulting structured control-tool surface.

What was not tested: no full end-to-end chat/channel agent turn with a live local Ollama daemon in this PR; this PR proves embedded runner config behavior, hosted Ollama structured-control compatibility, and changed-gate coverage.

target.title: fix(agents): count stream deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- Count streamed model response bytes from incremental delta payloads instead of serializing growing `partial` snapshots on every chunk.
- Keep non-delta diagnostic size accounting best-effort and omit `partial` from fallback envelope accounting.
- Add regressions proving diagnostic byte accounting does not touch full partial snapshots and does not break stream delivery when byte inspection fails.

## Linked context

Related https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Related https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87671 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87818.

This targets the diagnostics/full-partial contributor from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599#issuecomment-4555471713. It does not close the full Windows local-provider starvation issue by itself.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: model-call diagnostic byte accounting could repeatedly `JSON.stringify` stream chunks whose `partial` field held the full assistant answer so far, creating repeated work during dense text streams.
- Real environment tested: local Codex worktree for focused regression/static checks, plus AWS Crabbox Linux for the changed gate.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --reporter=dot

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts

.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local

node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 60m --ttl 120m --timing-json --shell -- "git fetch --unshallow origin || git fetch --deepen=2000 origin main; git fetch origin main:refs/remotes/origin/main; pnpm check:changed"
```

- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 2 files / 26 tests after the final rebase; oxfmt, oxlint, and diff whitespace checks passed; autoreview returned clean with no accepted/actionable findings; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_1d741a79db2c`, run `run_16a0aafc9846`, exit 0.
- Observed result after fix: text-delta diagnostics count only the new delta bytes and never invoke serialization on the growing `partial` snapshot; opaque chunk byte-accounting failures are ignored without dropping stream chunks.
- What was not tested: the original Windows local-daemon reproduction from #86599 was not rerun here. This is one fix in the broader event-loop starvation cluster, alongside the already-merged subscriber incremental streaming work and the separate native-Ollama dense-stream yielding PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the remote changed gate was run before the final conflict-free rebase onto the newest `origin/main`; after that rebase, the focused regression and static checks were rerun locally and still passed.
- Before evidence: commit c6e98493511b3a18e6f2f402693ef7dc832a071f introduced response stream byte accounting by serializing every streamed chunk, which is expensive when chunks include full `partial` snapshots.

## Tests and validation

Focused regression coverage was added in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts` for both the no-full-partial-serialization path and the best-effort opaque-chunk path.

Commands run are listed in Real behavior proof.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `No`; this changes diagnostic accounting work and the meaning of `responseStreamBytes` for delta chunks to represent incremental response bytes rather than repeated full snapshot envelopes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

Highest-risk area: downstream diagnostics consumers that compare `responseStreamBytes` values before/after this patch.

Risk mitigation: the field remains present, monotonic, and best-effort, but no longer scales with duplicated partial snapshots. Request payload byte accounting and terminal output-message capture remain unchanged.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

Waiting on: CI after PR creation.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: local autoreview found no accepted/actionable findings.

target.title: feat(trajectory): allow OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override for per-event byte cap
target.body:
## Summary

The per-event byte cap for trajectory recording (`TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES`) is
hard-coded to 256 KiB in two independent locations:

- `src/trajectory/paths.ts:8` — consumed by the core runtime writer
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.ts:35` — independent copy in the Codex app-server writer

This makes it impossible to raise the limit without rebuilding from source.
Heavy sessions (large `context.compiled` payloads, multi-tool responses) routinely
exceed 256 KiB and lose diagnostic data.

This PR:

1. Reads `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES` at startup in both writers,
   falling back to the current 256 KiB default.
2. Both core and Codex resolvers accept human-friendly suffixes (`512kb`, `2mb`, `1gb`)
or raw byte counts (`1048576`).
3. Adds unit tests in the Codex extension confirming the override and suffix paths.
4. Documents the new variable in `docs/tools/trajectory.md`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** trajectory events silently truncated at 256 KiB with no
user-configurable override.

**Environment tested:** Linux 6.8.0-106-generic (x64), OpenClaw v2026.5.7 (eeef486),
Node v24.14.1, 1212 trajectory files over 30 days.

**Steps run after the patch:**

```bash
grep -c '"truncated":true' ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.trajectory.jsonl | grep -v ':0$' | wc -l

node -e "const fs=require('fs');const d=fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8');const lines=d.split('
').filter(Boolean);const t=lines.filter(l=>{const o=JSON.parse(l);return o.data?.truncated});console.log('total:',lines.length,'truncated:',t.length)" ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/9e600bba-2bbe-4ff4-98ca-59ea5f1bb896.trajectory.jsonl
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before — same production host as §"Environment tested", current published `openclaw` build, last 30 days of sessions:

```text
$ grep -c '"truncated":true' ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.trajectory.jsonl | grep -v ':0$' | wc -l
459
```

A single heavy session (7.3 MB transcript) had 633 truncated events out of 1891 total — 33% data loss under the default 256 KiB cap.

After — patched build at PR head `76877f65f9c42b1154820dba43838ea413b3dc27`, a self-contained driver that wires the real `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` and records a synthetic oversized payload (60 keys × 5000 chars = ~293 KB after sanitization, deliberately under the inner `STRING_MAX_CHARS=32768` / `OBJECT_MAX_KEYS=64` / `DEPTH=6` limits so only the outer event-cap fires). The two phases use the **identical payload**; only the env var changes:

```text
==================================================
PHASE — default 256 KiB cap, identical payload
==================================================
>>> recorder.filePath = /root/openclaw-80681/pr80681-driver-1778935876980.trajectory.jsonl
>>> resolved env override: OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=(unset)
>>> payload: 60 keys × 5000 chars = 300000 chars total (~293 KB)
>>> recorded line bytes: 354
>>> truncated flag: true
>>> truncation originalBytes: 301095
>>> truncation limitBytes:    262144
>>> truncation reason:        trajectory-event-size-limit

==================================================
PHASE — OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb, identical payload
==================================================
>>> recorder.filePath = /root/openclaw-80681/pr80681-driver-1778935877004.trajectory.jsonl
>>> resolved env override: OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb
>>> payload: 60 keys × 5000 chars = 300000 chars total (~293 KB)
>>> recorded line bytes: 301095
>>> truncated flag: false
>>> payload key count preserved: 60
```

- Phase 1 (no override): the recorded `.trajectory.jsonl` line is replaced by a 354-byte sentinel carrying `truncated:true`, `originalBytes:301095`, `limitBytes:262144`, `reason:"trajectory-event-size-limit"`. This is the current `main` behavior under the default 256 KiB cap.
- Phase 2 (`OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb`): same payload, same recorder. The recorded line is the full **301095 bytes** and carries `truncated:false`; all 60 fields survive at full 5000 chars each.

The byte-for-byte difference (354 → 301095, ~880×) is the direct manifestation of the override raising the per-event cap above the actual payload size. The core-runtime fix at commit `76877f65...` resolves the cap once from `params.env` at recorder creation (ClawSweeper P2), so the override is honored across the recorder lifetime rather than being re-read from ambient `process.env` per event.

The full clawsweeper acceptance-criteria vitest set passes on the patched build:

```text
 RUN  v4.1.6

 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > returns the 256 KiB default when env is empty  125ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > returns the default when the override is whitespace only  1ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts raw byte counts  1ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts kb suffixes  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts mb suffixes  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts gb suffixes  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > falls back to the default on unparseable input  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > falls back to the default on non-positive input  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > module-load constant matches the empty-env resolver result  0ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > keeps write flags usable when O_NOFOLLOW is unavailable  9ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > records by default unless explicitly disabled  5ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > sanitizes session ids when resolving an override directory  3ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > honors explicit disablement  1ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > refuses to append through a symlinked parent directory  1ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > honors OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override  3ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > truncates events that exceed the runtime event byte limit  4ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > accepts human-friendly byte-size suffixes for the event cap override  2ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > falls back to default on invalid suffix values  2ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
   Duration  16.93s
```

The new test case `honors OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override` directly exercises the env-injection path at recorder creation.

**Observed result:** 459 out of 1212 trajectory files contain truncated events
(reason: `trajectory-event-size-limit`). A single heavy session
(`9e600bba`) has 633 truncated events out of 1891 total — 33% data loss.
After the patch, setting `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1048576`
eliminates truncation for sessions that fit within 1 MiB per event.
Setting `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=2mb` is equivalent.

**Not tested:** Filesystem-level I/O performance impact at very large per-event limits
(>10 MiB). The file-level cap (`TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_FILE_MAX_BYTES = 50 MiB`) still
applies and prevents unbounded disk growth.
## Addressing ClawSweeper review

### P2 — Resolve the core cap from the recorder env (`src/trajectory/runtime.ts:121`)

Fixed. `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` already captured `params.env` for `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY` enablement and trajectory path resolution; the truncation path was re-reading the per-event byte cap from ambient `process.env` on every event, which (a) silently ignored an injected `params.env` override and (b) let mid-session ambient mutations change behavior.

The truncation path now resolves the cap **once** at recorder creation from the captured `env`:

```ts
// inside createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder, after `env` is resolved
const eventMaxBytes = resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes(env);

// later, in buildEventLine
const boundedLine = truncateOversizedTrajectoryEvent(event, line, eventMaxBytes);
```

`truncateOversizedTrajectoryEvent` no longer calls `resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes()` internally; it accepts the pre-resolved `limitBytes` as a parameter. This matches the pinning semantics already used for `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY` and `resolveTrajectoryFilePath`.

### Prior review items (resolved earlier on this branch)

1. **F1 — broad SDK barrel import**: reverted to narrow subpaths.

   ```ts
   import { resolveUserPath } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime";
   import { appendRegularFile, resolveRegularFileAppendFlags } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/security-runtime";
   ```

2. **F2 — re-implemented file-write safety**: restored shared helpers. The hot append path uses `appendRegularFile()` and `resolveRegularFileAppendFlags()` from `security-runtime` (no local symlink/hardlink/nlink rewrite for the append path).

3. **F3 — invalid-fallback test using a valid value**: now uses a truly malformed value.

   ```ts
   env: { OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES: "abc-not-a-size" },
   ```

4. **F4 — CHANGELOG.md conflict**: rebased on latest `main`.

### Note on `lstatSync` calls in `writeTrajectoryPointerBestEffort`

Two `lstatSync(...).isSymbolicLink()` checks remain in `writeTrajectoryPointerBestEffort`. They are **not** related to the F2 finding:

- They guard the **trajectory pointer file** (`*.trajectory-path.json`), a separate sidecar JSON file written via `openSync` + `writeFileSync` to an attacker-untrusted path.
- The trajectory main file (`*.trajectory.jsonl`) append path uses `appendRegularFile()` and is unaffected.
- These pointer-specific symlink checks were added on this PR (no equivalent in current `main`) to harden the pointer write path. They are independent of the per-event byte cap feature, but are kept because removing them would lose the symlink protection on the pointer file.

If reviewers prefer the pointer-write symlink hardening to land in a follow-up PR rather than this one, happy to move them out — please advise.

target.title: fix(codex): refresh stale managed runtime plugin
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes the Codex stale-plugin convergence path behind https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650, including the duplicate evidence from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120.

What changes:

- Refreshes stale version-bound managed `@openclaw/codex` runtime packages through the trusted configured-plugin repair path when the installed package version is older than the running OpenClaw version.
- Leaves newer managed Codex packages alone so doctor does not downgrade a local/future install.
- Treats same-release beta-channel `@openclaw/codex@beta` companion packages as converged so repeated `doctor --fix` runs do not reinstall them.
- Restores `/status` usage lookup for bare `codex/gpt-*` routes by mapping Codex harness status auth through `openai-codex`.

## Linked context

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87650.

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88120. ClawSweeper closed that issue as a duplicate and asked that its stale-plugin-version, providerIds, config-backup, screenshot, and `codex/gpt-*` status evidence be folded into #87650.

Related but separate: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87851 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87436. That PR handles logical OpenAI session-route persistence after Codex-backed runs. This PR does not touch that persistence surface and is not intended as the repair for issue #87436. The changes are complementary: #87851 keeps durable session route state canonical, while this PR repairs stale managed Codex runtime package installs and the `codex/gpt-*` status/auth display path.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: stale managed `@openclaw/codex` packages could survive an OpenClaw update and leave the Codex runtime package behind the main app version, matching the reported `provider is not one of codex` failure evidence. A bare `codex/gpt-*` workaround route could also lose the Codex usage/auth status line because status looked up `codex` instead of the `openai-codex` auth provider.
- Real environment tested: Mantis native Telegram Desktop proof against this PR's `/status` path, plus local Windows source worktree proof on current PR head `99405a3b7c0d834ec0c0845b56caa5eab94d2dda` rebased onto current `main` through https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f6e1bc393b39c637e4b116b0d7b702676de5c869, including https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88137 and later main-CI fixes. No live VPS/user install was changed for this PR.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Mantis ran the `telegram-desktop-proof` scenario for `/status` on a `codex/gpt-5.5` route; local verification ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`, `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`, changed-file `oxlint`, changed-file `oxfmt --check`, and `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix: Mantis proof comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88135#issuecomment-4580013044 and artifact https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26662206002/artifacts/7301427679 show before/after native Telegram Desktop proof. Candidate proof shows the Codex OAuth status line restored for `/status` on `codex/gpt-5.5`; main proof omits that line. Motion clips are linked from the Mantis comment.
- Observed result after fix: native Telegram Desktop proof shows the candidate status card includes the Codex OAuth status line for the affected route. Focused doctor/status regression tests passed, the beta-channel second doctor pass now converges without reinstalling `@openclaw/codex@beta`, adjacent doctor Codex route warning tests passed, current-main import-cycle check passed, changed-file lint passed, formatting check passed, and whitespace diff check passed.
- Installed/package doctor proof after fix: captured on 2026-05-29 from a disposable local npm install of this PR package, without touching my live VPS/user install. I packed PR head `d082b78c6b1139936b47361020dbadf0404552f0`, installed the tarball into an isolated npm prefix, configured an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, set `update.channel=beta` and `agents.defaults.model=openai/gpt-5.5`, seeded a real stale managed `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.6` npm payload plus install record, then ran `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive`.
- Evidence from that installed/package proof: `openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (d082b78)`. Before doctor, the `codex` install record and installed `package.json` were both `2026.5.6`. Doctor reported `Installed missing configured plugin "codex" from @openclaw/codex@beta`. After doctor, the `codex` install record had `source=npm`, `spec=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedName=@openclaw/codex`, `resolvedVersion=2026.5.28-beta.4`, `resolvedSpec=@openclaw/codex@2026.5.28-beta.4`, and the installed package payload was `@openclaw/codex 2026.5.28-beta.4`.
- Follow-up convergence proof after ClawSweeper review: on source head `99405a3b7c0d834ec0c0845b56caa5eab94d2dda`, added regression coverage where the first beta-channel doctor pass repairs stale `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.6` to `2026.5.28-beta.4`, then a second doctor pass makes no install call and returns no changes.
- Note on package channel: at capture time npm reported `@openclaw/codex` dist-tags `latest=2026.5.27` and `beta=2026.5.28-beta.4`; beta channel was used so the managed Codex runtime package refreshed onto the current beta companion line for the PR package.
- What was not tested: no live VPS/user install was changed for this installed-package proof; the disposable temp state, npm prefix, and npm cache were deleted after capture.

## Verification

Passed locally after the latest follow-up commit:

- `src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`: 63 tests passed, including beta-channel second-pass convergence.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`: 23 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`: 17 tests passed.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`: 104 tests passed.
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts`: 0 cycles.
- `pnpm exec oxlint ...changed files...`: passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...changed files...`: passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`: passed.

Known local proof gap:

- `src/plugins/update.test.ts` has one unrelated local Windows failure in `reuses the recorded managed extensions root when updating external plugins`; no files in that test surface are changed by this PR.

target.title: fix(imessage): preserve SMS approval reply routes
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserves SMS-qualified iMessage routes across inbound context, session route storage, approval updates, and async follow-up delivery.
- Lets bare direct iMessage/SMS handle targets inherit the configured account service, while still honoring explicit `sms:`, `imessage:`, and `auto:` prefixes.
- Uses the configured iMessage probe timeout for live sends so the RPC path does not fall back to the shorter default timeout during approval delivery.
- Keeps the change scoped to the iMessage plugin; no `src/infra/*` files are touched.

## Linked context

Related #87770

Requested by maintainer in the PR isolation workflow.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Direct SMS iMessage approval flows should deliver the approval request, approval acknowledgement, and final agent follow-up as SMS instead of falling back to `imessage:<phone>` or failing threaded reply delivery.

Real environment tested: Local macOS Messages database and Google Voice in Safari, using `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=prod` against the prod OpenClaw profile. The live SMS handle and phone numbers are intentionally redacted from this PR body.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
git diff --check
OPENCLAW_RUN_NODE_SKIP_DTS_BUILD=1 OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs gatewayWatch
OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_LOCK=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/imessage/src/channel.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/monitor-provider.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/targets.ts
OPENCLAW_PROFILE=prod node dist/index.js channels status --channel imessage --probe
OPENCLAW_PROFILE=prod node dist/index.js gateway --port 18789
```

Live proof steps:

```text
1. Sent a Google Voice SMS prompt asking OpenClaw to run: touch /tmp/openclaw-imsg-fix-160123
2. Waited for the native Exec approval request over the iMessage channel.
3. Replied from Google Voice: /approve d73596e7-e7d7-41e1-a160-8788a261e5e4 allow-once
4. Verified the command side effect file existed.
5. Verified the approval request, acknowledgement, and final completion message were all Messages DB `SMS` rows with `error=0`.
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
Messages rows >= 66324:
66324 | inbound prompt       | SMS | error=0 | nonce imsg-fix-160123
66325 | approval request     | SMS | error=0 | Exec approval required, id d73596e7-e7d7-41e1-a160-8788a261e5e4
66326 | inbound approval     | SMS | error=0 | /approve d73596e7-e7d7-41e1-a160-8788a261e5e4 allow-once
66327 | approval ack         | SMS | error=0 | Approval allow-once submitted
66328 | final agent followup | SMS | error=0 | Command ran successfully. Exit code: 0

Command side effect:
-rw-r--r--@ 1 kevinlin wheel 0 May 29 16:03 /tmp/openclaw-imsg-fix-160123

Gateway log:
16:01:55 exec.approval.request succeeded
16:03:28 exec.approval.waitDecision succeeded
16:03:29 agent follow-up started for approval d73596e7-e7d7-41e1-a160-8788a261e5e4
16:03:36 Command ran successfully. Exit code: `0`.
```

Observed result after fix: The direct SMS approval request, `/approve` acknowledgement, and final async exec follow-up all stayed on SMS with `error=0`, and the requested command created `/tmp/openclaw-imsg-fix-160123`.

What was not tested: Unit tests were not run per maintainer instruction for this live integration proof. WhatsApp was not tested for this PR.

Before evidence: On the main checkout before this fix, the same prod-profile SMS approval path logged iMessage delivery failures such as `exec approvals: failed to deliver ... OutboundDeliveryError: imsg rpc timeout (send)`, and the expected `/tmp/openclaw-imsg-main-*` command side-effect files were absent.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```sh
git diff --check
OPENCLAW_RUN_NODE_SKIP_DTS_BUILD=1 OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs gatewayWatch
OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_LOCK=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/imessage/src/channel.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/monitor-provider.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/targets.ts
```

Live integration proof:

```text
iMessage/SMS prod approval proof passed for nonce imsg-fix-160123.
```

Regression coverage added or updated: None. The requested proof for this change was live iMessage/SMS integration behavior, not unit coverage.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Direct SMS iMessage approval replies and follow-ups should now remain SMS-qualified.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The iMessage direct SMS approval route and resumed async follow-up path.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change preserves explicit service prefixes, derives implicit direct routes from the configured iMessage account service, and keeps chat/group targets on the existing chat route path.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Run CI and re-review the updated iMessage SMS route-preservation diff with the live proof above.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer/bot review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

ClawSweeper's proof blocker was addressed with redacted live iMessage/SMS approval proof.

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- Rolling timer suffix shows elapsed time while running, cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Depends on

- #80046 (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args)
- #85381 (CLI thinking_delta events, stacked on #80046)

**Merge order is safe.** The feature is default-off and consumes only existing callbacks. With the interactive backend's restored thinking, reasoning arrives on `onReasoningStream`; without #85381 (thinking redacted), CLI reasoning simply never reaches the renderer and the lane shows tool/event lines without CLI-native reasoning — degrading gracefully rather than breaking. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected, so this can merge before or after #85381.

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (unwrap `<think>` markers, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, defensive final-answer strip, reasoning-tag unwrapping (incl. literal tags inside code preserved)
- [ ] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Live proof (current head)

_Redacted current-head Telegram proof for the cases below. (The earlier screenshot predated the reply-out / continuation / empty-flush changes and is superseded.)_

- **Normal interleaving** — reasoning + tool-use lines in the Thinking lane:
  <!-- add screenshot -->
- **No duplicate final answer** — the reply appears once, as its own message, not inside the Thinking lane:
  <!-- add screenshot -->
- **Long-message continuation** — a long turn spills the Thinking lane into a second message instead of freezing:
  <!-- add screenshot -->
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn with no reasoning/tool content emits no bare "Thinking" message:
  <!-- add screenshot -->

target.title: fix(copilot): drop thinking blocks from replay
target.body:
## Summary
- strip thinking blocks from GitHub Copilot Claude replay history instead of preserving the latest assistant reasoning turn
- apply the same Copilot Claude stripping rule to the request-time Anthropic stream payload wrapper used for tool continuations
- keep the existing default latest-assistant preservation behavior for other providers/callers
- cover Copilot replay cases for text, thinking-only turns, tool calls, and request-time payload replay

Fixes #81520

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
GitHub Copilot Claude should not receive prior assistant `thinking` or `redacted_thinking` blocks when OpenClaw replays session history or sends a tool-continuation request payload, because Copilot rejects those replayed blocks.

Real environment tested:
WSL2 Ubuntu checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-refresh-87060`, PR head `f31f6c771a1`, source-level runtime payload harness through the real `wrapCopilotAnthropicStream` request wrapper, plus focused embedded-agent replay tests. No live Copilot account was available in this WSL environment.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/github-copilot/stream.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts extensions/github-copilot/provider-runtime.contract.test.ts --reporter=dot`
2. `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/github-copilot/stream.ts extensions/github-copilot/stream.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts`
3. `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/github-copilot/stream.ts extensions/github-copilot/stream.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts`
4. `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
5. `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix:
Vitest output: 5 files passed, 179 tests passed. Formatter output: all matched files use the correct format. Oxlint output: found 0 warnings and 0 errors. Diff whitespace checks produced no output. The new `wrapCopilotAnthropicStream` regression tests capture the real outgoing Copilot Anthropic payload after `options.onPayload` and assert mixed assistant content is reduced to visible text while thinking-only assistant replay messages are dropped before tool-result continuation replay.

Observed result after fix:
The request-time Copilot Anthropic payload wrapper now removes both `thinking` and `redacted_thinking` assistant content before applying cache markers. A tool-continuation payload with a thinking-only assistant replay message is sent without that assistant message, while surrounding user and tool-result messages remain intact.

What was not tested:
Redacted live Copilot Claude proof from a real authenticated Copilot account is still missing; no Copilot auth files were available in this WSL environment.

## Validation
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/github-copilot/stream.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts extensions/github-copilot/provider-runtime.contract.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 5 files, 179 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/github-copilot/stream.ts extensions/github-copilot/stream.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/github-copilot/stream.ts extensions/github-copilot/stream.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`

## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`

- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Current head: `cee6492154` (`cee649215411440a0fe083c94ce99e8f5516bdfa`).

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy for Telegram forum-topic media pre-download gating, plus current CI guard regressions for shrinkwrap check context and Azure Speech env-restore security scanning.
- **Real environment tested:** PR checkout on Patrick's OpenClaw workspace using Node 24.13.0 / npm 11.6.2 for shrinkwrap parity, and the repository's real Telegram mention-policy proof harness against the changed runtime sources.

commands.author: pfrederiksen

target.title: Allow agents to harden bundled plugin discovery
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86136.

This PR allows agents to apply one specific doctor-recommended hardening change through the agent-facing gateway config tool:

```json
{
  "plugins": {
    "bundledDiscovery": "allowlist"
  }
}
```

It does not generally open plugin discovery config to agents. The new guard allows only the hardening value `"allowlist"` and still rejects attempts to set or restore legacy `"compat"` mode.

## Problem

`openclaw doctor` recommends `plugins.bundledDiscovery: "allowlist"` when an installation uses a restrictive `plugins.allow`. That recommendation matters because legacy `"compat"` bundled discovery can keep omitted bundled providers discoverable even when an operator is trying to restrict plugin exposure.

The config schema also presents `plugins.bundledDiscovery` as a valid hot-reloadable enum:

```text
path: plugins.bundledDiscovery
enum: ["compat", "allowlist"]
reloadKind: hot
```

Before this PR, an agent could see the schema and doctor recommendation, but could not apply the hardening patch:

```text
gateway config.patch cannot change protected config paths: plugins.bundledDiscovery
```

That forced manual config-file editing for a security-positive change.

## Root Cause

The agent gateway tool protects config mutation by computing changed config paths and rejecting paths that are not listed in `ALLOWED_GATEWAY_CONFIG_PATHS`. `plugins.bundledDiscovery` was not in that list, so the guard blocked both directions equally:

- blocked `compat -> allowlist`, which is hardening
- blocked `allowlist -> compat`, which is loosening

The desired behavior needs a path exception plus a value-direction guard, not a broad plugin config exemption.

## Implementation

This PR changes two files:

- `src/agents/tools/gateway-tool.ts`
  - adds `plugins.bundledDiscovery` to the agent-facing config path allowlist
  - adds an explicit value guard that permits only `"allowlist"`
  - rejects any agent-facing attempt to loosen bundled discovery back to `"compat"`
- `src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts`
  - verifies the path is included in guard coverage
  - verifies `compat -> allowlist` is allowed
  - verifies `allowlist -> compat` is blocked

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Agent-facing gateway `config.patch` hardening for `plugins.bundledDiscovery`. Agents should be able to apply doctor-recommended hardening to `"allowlist"`, while attempts to loosen back to `"compat"` remain blocked.

Real environment tested: Sanitized live OpenClaw `2026.5.22` installation with restrictive `plugins.allow` and `plugins.bundledDiscovery: "compat"`, plus this PR branch at `890a60aebcfc11b11785c079f8f0e2086336b9e2`. The live gateway was running as a Linux user service. No tokens, chat IDs, private channel names, user content, private account identifiers, hostnames, or local user paths are included.

Before-fix evidence: The live published setup exposed the failure before this PR. Schema lookup showed `plugins.bundledDiscovery` as a hot-reloadable enum, but the agent-facing patch was rejected by the protected-path guard before the gateway RPC.

```text
schema lookup:
path: plugins.bundledDiscovery
enum: ["compat", "allowlist"]
reloadKind: hot

agent-facing patch:
{"plugins":{"bundledDiscovery":"allowlist"}}

result:
gateway config.patch cannot change protected config paths: plugins.bundledDiscovery
```

Exact steps or command run after the patch: Ran this PR branch's agent-facing `config.patch` mutation guard directly against sanitized config snapshots for both directions, then ran the focused guard test file.

```text
GIT_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) node --import tsx <agent-facing config.patch guard probe>
npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the PR branch showing the changed `config.patch` guard accepts `"allowlist"` and rejects `"compat"`, followed by focused test output.

```text
PR branch: 890a60aebcfc11b11785c079f8f0e2086336b9e2
Command: node --import tsx <agent-facing config.patch guard probe>
agent-facing config.patch compat -> allowlist: accepted
agent-facing config.patch allowlist -> compat: rejected: gateway config.patch cannot loosen bundled plugin discovery; only plugins.bundledDiscovery="allowlist" is allowed

npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       43 passed (43)
```

Current-head validation rerun: After rebasing, I reran the focused guard coverage on the current PR head.

```text
git rev-parse HEAD
890a60aebcfc11b11785c079f8f0e2086336b9e2

npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       43 passed (43)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 15.46s
```

Observed result after fix: On the PR branch, the exact agent-facing `config.patch` guard now permits the hardening transition `compat -> allowlist` and rejects the loosening transition `allowlist -> compat`. The published live setup confirmed `plugins.bundledDiscovery: "allowlist"` is the correct operational target because it cleared the doctor warning after manual workaround and restart.

What was not tested: I did not replace the live installed gateway service with this PR branch. The PR-branch proof exercises the changed agent-facing `config.patch` guard directly with sanitized config snapshots; the live gateway proof uses the published `2026.5.22` package to confirm the pre-fix failure and operational effect of the target value.

## Validation

```text
npm test -- --run src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
1 file passed
43 tests passed
```

GitHub checks are currently passing or intentionally skipped for this PR, including the real behavior proof gate.

## Security / Risk

Risk is low and intentionally bounded.

This is not a broad plugin-configuration bypass. The PR allows a single security-positive value through the agent-facing guard and rejects the loosening value:

- `compat -> allowlist` is allowed
- `allowlist -> compat` is blocked
- any other protected config paths remain protected by the existing guard

The main security concern is accidentally letting agents re-enable legacy bundled discovery. The added value guard and tests cover that explicitly.

## Privacy / Sanitization

The linked issue, PR body, and comments are sanitized. They do not include tokens, chat IDs, private channel names, personal data, local usernames, private hostnames, or user content. Public GitHub issue/PR links and generic OpenClaw config keys are included because they are necessary to review and reproduce the fix.

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- Rolling timer suffix shows elapsed time while running, cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Depends on

- #80046 (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args)
- #85381 (CLI thinking_delta events, stacked on #80046)

**Merge order is safe.** The feature is default-off and consumes only existing callbacks. With the interactive backend's restored thinking, reasoning arrives on `onReasoningStream`; without #85381 (thinking redacted), CLI reasoning simply never reaches the renderer and the lane shows tool/event lines without CLI-native reasoning — degrading gracefully rather than breaking. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected, so this can merge before or after #85381.

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (unwrap `<think>` markers, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, defensive final-answer strip, reasoning-tag unwrapping (incl. literal tags inside code preserved)
- [ ] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Live proof (current head)

_Redacted current-head Telegram proof for the cases below. (The earlier screenshot predated the reply-out / continuation / empty-flush changes and is superseded.)_

- **Normal interleaving** — reasoning + tool-use lines in the Thinking lane:
  <!-- add screenshot -->
- **No duplicate final answer** — the reply appears once, as its own message, not inside the Thinking lane:
  <!-- add screenshot -->
- **Long-message continuation** — a long turn spills the Thinking lane into a second message instead of freezing:
  <!-- add screenshot -->
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn with no reasoning/tool content emits no bare "Thinking" message:
  <!-- add screenshot -->

## Summary

- lets Zen and Go OpenCode plugins use separate env vars
- documents fallback order for `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY`, `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY`, and shared `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- formats the Go provider docs table so docs checks do not fail on this PR diff

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: OpenCode Zen and Go can be configured independently while retaining shared `OPENCODE_API_KEY` fallback behavior.

Real environment tested: macOS checkout of this PR branch, Node/pnpm repo toolchain, dependencies installed from the lockfile.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx -e 'const load=async(path)=>{const plugin=(await import(path)).default; const providers=[]; plugin.register({registerProvider:(p)=>providers.push(p),registerMediaUnderstandingProvider:()=>{}}); return providers[0];}; const zen=await load("./extensions/opencode/index.ts"); const go=await load("./extensions/opencode-go/index.ts"); console.log(JSON.stringify({zen:{provider:zen.id,envVars:zen.envVars},go:{provider:go.id,envVars:go.envVars}},null,2));'
pnpm check:docs
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
node --import tsx ...
{
  "zen": {
    "provider": "opencode",
    "envVars": [
      "OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY",
      "OPENCODE_API_KEY"
    ]
  },
  "go": {
    "provider": "opencode-go",
    "envVars": [
      "OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY",
      "OPENCODE_API_KEY",
      "OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY"
    ]
  }
}

Docs formatting clean (650 files).
markdownlint Summary: 0 error(s)
Docs MDX check passed
docs:check-i18n-glossary failed on unrelated changed docs not present in this PR diff:
docs/cli/tui.md
docs/plugins/reference/codex-supervisor.md
docs/specs/claw-supervisor.md
docs/tools/goal.md
```

Observed result after fix: the runtime plugin registrations expose separate Zen and Go env vars with shared fallback ordering; the OpenCode Go provider page no longer has the markdown table formatting drift that caused this PR's docs failure.

What was not tested: no live OpenCode API call was run because this environment has no OpenCode key available; the remaining docs glossary failure is unrelated to this PR's changed files.

## Verification

```bash
pnpm install --offline
pnpm check:docs
git diff --name-only upstream/main...HEAD
```

node --import tsx ...
{
  "provider": "opencode",

target.title: fix(config): cap runtime session-store cache retention
target.body:
# Fix: cap runtime session-store cache retention for oversized stores

## Summary

This patch adds a runtime byte cap to the live session-store object and snapshot caches so oversized `sessions.json` loads do not stay resident as parsed object graphs in the gateway heap.

The on-disk session store remains the source of truth. Large stores can still be reloaded on demand, but they are no longer retained in memory just because they were loaded once.

Scope note: this PR only covers the session-store work in this branch. It does not include the separate bootstrap-cache patch or the separate `webgate` patch from the other branch.

## Issue Evidence

- The gateway previously crashed with a V8 heap OOM on the same machine.
- Before the patch, the live session-store tree and quarantine tree were multi-gigabyte, and the runtime was repeatedly loading the full session store during startup/recovery paths.
- The old behavior kept parsed session-store object graphs and immutable snapshots in memory after load, which is the retention pattern this patch caps.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed: oversized session-store hydration could retain parsed object graphs and snapshots in the live gateway cache, which increased heap pressure during startup, recovery, and repeated session access.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw workspace on the same machine, using the source tree at `/root/.openclaw/openclaw-fork-rebase` and the same runtime files that back the gateway.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- I ran a local `tsx` script against the source tree at `/root/.openclaw/openclaw-fork-rebase`.
- The script created a temporary `sessions.json`, forced `OPENCLAW_SESSION_CACHE_MAX_BYTES=64`, loaded the store twice, and probed the serialized and snapshot caches.
- The temp store used a single oversized session entry; the raw payload is omitted here because the proof only needs cache behavior, not session content.

Evidence after fix:
```text
after save serialized entries= 0
after save snapshot entries= 0
storeReads= 2
sameReference= false
equalPayload= true
serialized entries after loads= 0
snapshot entries after loads= 0
small serialized entries= 1
oversize direct read null= true
after oversized direct read serialized entries= 0
after oversized direct read snapshot entries= 0
```

Observed result after fix: for an oversized store, `0` cache entries is the correct result because the patch intentionally keeps that store out of the runtime caches. Repeated loads still return correct data, and direct oversized cache reads invalidate instead of retaining the object graph in memory. The `small serialized entries= 1` line is the positive control that proves normal-sized stores can still enter the cache. In the live gateway, the process stayed healthy and recovered back down after spikes during normal use; the conservative internal "critical" warnings still fired at times, but the post-GC floor remained bounded in the observed window and did not show the earlier runaway crash pattern.

User observation: under heavy normal use, the gateway also seemed noticeably faster after this patch, with smoother response under load. That is currently anecdotal only and not backed by measurement yet, so it should be treated as a qualitative observation rather than proof.

What was not tested: an overnight soak or a full quarantine/restart-recovery cleanup pass after this specific patch. Supplemental runtime data can be added later if the long-run floor starts drifting.

## Before-Fix Evidence (Supplementary)

<details>
<summary>Pre-patch crash and pressure symptoms</summary>

- The gateway previously crashed with a V8 heap OOM rather than a native fault.
- The live process had repeated memory-pressure warnings before this patch.
- The on-disk session corpus was large enough to justify avoiding persistent in-memory retention of oversized stores.

</details>

target.title: fix(agents): cap bootstrap snapshot cache
target.body:
# Fix: cap bootstrap-cache retention per session key

## Summary

This patch caps the in-memory bootstrap-cache so the gateway does not retain an unbounded number of loaded workspace bootstrap bundles per session key.

The on-disk bootstrap files remain the source of truth. Cached bundles can still be reused while active, but older session keys are evicted instead of remaining resident forever.

Scope note: this PR only covers the bootstrap-cache work in this branch. It does not include the separate session-store patch or the separate `webgate` patch from the other branch.

## Issue Evidence

- The bootstrap loader retained one loaded bundle per distinct session key with no eviction.
- Under session churn, that made the bootstrap cache a persistent in-memory accumulator instead of a bounded reuse layer.
- The cache retention problem was separate from the large session-store hydration issue.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed: bootstrap-cache retention could grow without bound across distinct session keys, increasing resident memory during long-lived gateway operation.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw workspace on the same machine, using the source tree at `/root/.openclaw/openclaw-fork-rebase`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import {
  clearAllBootstrapSnapshots,
  getBootstrapSnapshotCacheSizeForTest,
  getOrLoadBootstrapFiles,
  hasBootstrapSnapshotForTest,
} from './src/agents/bootstrap-cache.ts';

clearAllBootstrapSnapshots();
console.log('initial size=', getBootstrapSnapshotCacheSizeForTest());

for (let i = 0; i < 65; i += 1) {
  await getOrLoadBootstrapFiles({ workspaceDir: process.cwd(), sessionKey: `session-${i}` });
}

console.log('after 65 unique session keys size=', getBootstrapSnapshotCacheSizeForTest());
console.log('session-0 retained=', hasBootstrapSnapshotForTest('session-0'));

await getOrLoadBootstrapFiles({ workspaceDir: process.cwd(), sessionKey: 'session-0' });
console.log('after reloading session-0 size=', getBootstrapSnapshotCacheSizeForTest());
console.log('session-0 retained after reload=', hasBootstrapSnapshotForTest('session-0'));
EOF
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
initial size=0
after 65 unique session keys size=64
session-0 retained=false
after reloading session-0 size=64
session-0 retained after reload=true
```

Observed result after fix: the bootstrap-cache stays bounded, oldest entries are evicted when the cap is exceeded, and active session keys can still be reloaded without growing the cache past the limit.

What was not tested: an overnight soak specifically isolated to bootstrap-cache churn.

## Before-Fix Evidence (Supplementary)

<details>
<summary>Pre-patch retention symptoms</summary>

- The old behavior had no eviction bound for distinct session keys.
- That meant each new session key could add another resident bootstrap bundle.
- This was a separate retention layer from the session-store cache.

</details>

target.title: fix(discord): accumulate reasoning progress deltas
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83983.

Discord progress-mode reasoning updates were formatting each reasoning callback with the display `Thinking...` wrapper before draft-preview merge logic saw it. Since the merge logic treats `Thinking...`/`Reasoning:` prefixed text as a full snapshot, Codex app-server delta chunks like `"Considering"`, `" plugin"`, `" installation"`, `"!"` could replace each other until only the final tiny chunk remained visible.

This keeps reasoning stream payloads raw until `pushReasoningProgress()` merges delta/snapshot semantics, then applies the existing reasoning display formatter once for the stable progress line.

No config surface or plugin surface changes.

## Proof

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts --reporter=dot
# Test Files 1 passed (1)
# Tests 77 passed (77)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-utils.test.ts --reporter=dot
# Test Files 2 passed (2)
# Tests 117 passed (117)

./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.draft-preview.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts
# All matched files use the correct format.

./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.draft-preview.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts
# Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
# passed

git diff --check
# passed

pnpm check:changed
# passed
```
